553	
554	def evaluate_rules(
555	    metrics: Sequence[DecisionMetric],
556	    *,
557	    market_closed_reason: str | None,
558	    sources_count: int,
559	    min_distinct_sources: int,
560	    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
561	) -> tuple[tuple[DecisionRuleResult, ...], MarketDecisionAction, str | None, list[str]]:
562	    """Evaluate the §2.3 rule graph and return (rules, action, side, reasons).
563	
564	    Ordering matters: vetoes are evaluated first and short-circuit
565	    later rules (their ``fired`` is False). Bucket rules then choose
566	    a candidate action which threshold/combiner rules may downgrade.
567	    """
568	    metric_by_name = {m.name: m for m in metrics}
569	    edge = metric_by_name["market_mispricing_edge"]
570	    confidence = metric_by_name["calibration_adjusted_confidence"]
571	    locality = metric_by_name["source_domain_locality"]
572	    contradiction = metric_by_name["contradiction_pressure"]
573	    liquidity = metric_by_name["liquidity_cost_feasibility"]
574	    decay = metric_by_name["temporal_decay_pressure"]
575	
576	    rules: list[DecisionRuleResult] = []
577	    reasons: list[str] = []
578	    veto_hit = False
579	
580	    # Veto 1: market closed/expired
581	    rule_closed = DecisionRuleResult(
582	        name="market_open",
583	        kind="veto",
584	        fired=True,
585	        passed=market_closed_reason is None,
586	        detail=market_closed_reason or "open",
587	    )
588	    rules.append(rule_closed)
589	    if not rule_closed.passed:
590	        reasons.append(rule_closed.detail)
591	        veto_hit = True
592	
593	    # Veto 2: insufficient distinct sources
594	    rule_sources = DecisionRuleResult(
595	        name="distinct_source_floor",
596	        kind="veto",
597	        fired=not veto_hit,
598	        passed=sources_count >= min_distinct_sources,
599	        detail=f"{sources_count}/{min_distinct_sources} distinct sources",
600	    )
601	    rules.append(rule_sources)
602	    if rule_sources.fired and not rule_sources.passed:
603	        reasons.append(rule_sources.detail)
604	        veto_hit = True
605	
606	    # Veto 3: high contradiction pressure
607	    rule_contradict = DecisionRuleResult(
608	        name="contradiction_veto",
609	        kind="veto",
610	        fired=not veto_hit,
611	        passed=contradiction.value < CONTRADICTION_VETO,
612	        detail=f"contradiction_pressure={contradiction.value:.3f} vs τ={CONTRADICTION_VETO}",
613	    )
614	    rules.append(rule_contradict)
615	    if rule_contradict.fired and not rule_contradict.passed:
616	        reasons.append(rule_contradict.detail)
617	        veto_hit = True
618	
619	    # Veto 4: temporal decay catastrophic
620	    rule_decay_veto = DecisionRuleResult(
621	        name="temporal_decay_veto",
622	        kind="veto",
623	        fired=not veto_hit,
624	        passed=decay.value < DECAY_VETO,
625	        detail=f"temporal_decay_pressure={decay.value:.3f} vs τ={DECAY_VETO}",
626	    )
627	    rules.append(rule_decay_veto)
628	    if rule_decay_veto.fired and not rule_decay_veto.passed:
629	        reasons.append(rule_decay_veto.detail)
630	        veto_hit = True
631	
632	    # Bucket: edge magnitude
633	    abs_edge = abs(edge.value) if not edge.low_confidence else 0.0
634	    side = "YES" if edge.value >= 0 else "NO"
635	    if veto_hit:
636	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
637	        bucket_detail = "veto upstream → abstain"
638	        bucket_fired = False
639	    elif edge.low_confidence:
640	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
641	        bucket_detail = "edge data missing → abstain"
642	        bucket_fired = True
643	    elif abs_edge < EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD:
644	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH
645	        bucket_detail = f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} below paper threshold {EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD}"
646	        bucket_fired = True
647	    elif abs_edge < EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD:
648	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE
649	        bucket_detail = (
650	            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} clears paper, below live threshold {EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
651	        )
652	        bucket_fired = True
653	    else:
654	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE
655	        bucket_detail = f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} clears live threshold {EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
656	        bucket_fired = True
657	    rules.append(
658	        DecisionRuleResult(
659	            name="edge_bucket",
660	            kind="bucket",
661	            fired=bucket_fired,
662	            passed=bucket_fired,
663	            detail=bucket_detail,
664	        )
665	    )
666	
667	    # Liquidity gate (downgrades LIVE_CANDIDATE → PAPER_TRADE,
668	    # downgrades PAPER_TRADE → WATCH when liquidity is zero).
669	    liquidity_ok = liquidity.value >= LIQUIDITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not liquidity.low_confidence
670	    rule_liquidity = DecisionRuleResult(
671	        name="liquidity_threshold",
672	        kind="threshold",
673	        fired=not veto_hit,
674	        passed=liquidity_ok,
675	        detail=f"liquidity_cost_feasibility={liquidity.value:.3f} vs τ={LIQUIDITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD}",
676	    )
677	    rules.append(rule_liquidity)
678	    if rule_liquidity.fired:
679	        if liquidity.value <= 0.0 and not liquidity.low_confidence:
680	            if bucket_action in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE):
681	                reasons.append("liquidity collapsed → watch")
682	                bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH
683	        elif not liquidity_ok and bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE:
684	            reasons.append("liquidity below live threshold → paper")
685	            bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE
686	
687	    # Confidence/locality gate for live escalation only.
688	    confidence_ok = (
689	        confidence.value >= CONFIDENCE_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not confidence.low_confidence
690	    )
691	    locality_ok = locality.value >= LOCALITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not locality.low_confidence
692	    rule_conf = DecisionRuleResult(
693	        name="confidence_threshold",
694	        kind="threshold",
695	        fired=not veto_hit,
696	        passed=confidence_ok,
697	        detail=(
698	            f"calibration_adjusted_confidence={confidence.value:.3f} vs τ={CONFIDENCE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
699	            + (" (low_confidence)" if confidence.low_confidence else "")
700	        ),
701	    )
702	    rule_locality = DecisionRuleResult(
703	        name="locality_threshold",
704	        kind="threshold",
705	        fired=not veto_hit,
706	        passed=locality_ok,
707	        detail=(
708	            f"source_domain_locality={locality.value:.3f} vs τ={LOCALITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
709	            + (" (low_confidence)" if locality.low_confidence else "")
710	        ),
711	    )
712	    rules.append(rule_conf)
713	    rules.append(rule_locality)
714	    if bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE:
715	        if not (confidence_ok and locality_ok):
716	            reasons.append("confidence or locality below live thresholds → paper")
717	            bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE
718	
719	    # Moderate decay downgrade
720	    if decay.value >= DECAY_DOWNGRADE and bucket_action in (
721	        MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE,
722	        MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE,
723	    ):
724	        reasons.append(
725	            f"temporal_decay_pressure={decay.value:.3f} ≥ {DECAY_DOWNGRADE} → watch"
726	        )
727	        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH
728	
729	    # Position-state overlays. v1 only emits REDUCE/EXIT when the
730	    # caller declares an open position whose side disagrees with the
731	    # current edge or whose underlying principle is revoked.
732	    state = _position_state(position_state)
733	    if state:
734	        open_side = str(state.get("open_side", "") or "").upper()
735	        revoked = bool(state.get("principles_revoked", False))
736	        position_action: MarketDecisionAction | None = None
737	        position_detail = ""
738	        if revoked:
739	            position_action = MarketDecisionAction.EXIT
740	            position_detail = "an active principle was revoked → exit"
741	        elif open_side and open_side != side and not edge.low_confidence:
742	            position_action = MarketDecisionAction.REDUCE
743	            position_detail = (
744	                f"existing {open_side} position vs edge side {side} → reduce"
745	            )
746	        rules.append(
747	            DecisionRuleResult(
748	                name="position_overlay",
749	                kind="combiner",
750	                fired=True,
751	                passed=position_action is not None,
752	                detail=position_detail or "no overlay applied",
753	            )
754	        )
755	        if position_action is not None:
756	            reasons.append(position_detail)
757	            bucket_action = position_action
758	
759	    if bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN and not reasons:
760	        reasons.append("rule graph produced no actionable decision")
761	    if bucket_action in (MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN,) or veto_hit:
762	        side_out = None
763	    else:
764	        side_out = side
765	    return tuple(rules), bucket_action, side_out, reasons
766	
767	
768	def build_decision_trace(
769	    *,
770	    market: Any,
771	    sources: Sequence[Any],
772	    citations: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
773	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
774	    paper_bet: Any | None = None,
775	    calibration_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
776	    extra_contradictions: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] = (),
777	    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
778	    now: datetime | None = None,
779	    min_distinct_sources: int = 3,
780	) -> MarketDecisionTrace:
781	    """Assemble metrics, evaluate the rule graph, return a trace.
782	
783	    The trace's ``confidence`` is the geometric mean of the
784	    calibration-adjusted confidence and ``(1 - contradiction_pressure)``,
785	    pulled down by ``low_confidence`` metrics so missing data is
786	    visible in the scalar that the UI surfaces.
787	    """
788	    now = _as_utc(now or datetime.now(timezone.utc))
789	    probability_yes = payload.get("probability_yes")
790	    market_yes_price = getattr(market, "current_yes_price", None)
791	
792	    edge_metric = compute_market_mispricing_edge(probability_yes, market_yes_price)
793	    confidence_metric = compute_calibration_adjusted_confidence(
794	        payload.get("confidence_low"),
795	        payload.get("confidence_high"),
796	        calibration_state,
797	        topic_hint=str(payload.get("topic_hint") or "").strip() or None,
798	    )
799	    locality_metric = compute_source_domain_locality(sources, citations, market)
800	    contradiction_metric = compute_contradiction_pressure(citations, extra_contradictions)
801	    decay_metric = compute_temporal_decay_pressure(sources, market, now=now)
802	
803	    suggested_stake: float | None = None
804	    if paper_bet is not None:
805	        raw_stake = getattr(paper_bet, "stake_usd", None)
806	        if raw_stake is not None:
807	            try:
808	                suggested_stake = float(Decimal(str(raw_stake)))
809	            except (InvalidOperation, TypeError, ValueError):
810	                suggested_stake = None
811	    liquidity_metric = compute_liquidity_cost_feasibility(market, suggested_stake)
812	
813	    metrics: tuple[DecisionMetric, ...] = (
814	        edge_metric,
815	        confidence_metric,
816	        locality_metric,
817	        contradiction_metric,
818	        liquidity_metric,
819	        decay_metric,
820	    )
821	
822	    closed, closed_reason = _market_is_closed(market, now)
823	    rules, action, side, reasons = evaluate_rules(
824	        metrics,
825	        market_closed_reason=closed_reason if closed else None,
826	        sources_count=len(sources),
827	        min_distinct_sources=min_distinct_sources,
828	        position_state=position_state,
829	    )
830	
831	    confidence_components = [
832	        confidence_metric.value if not confidence_metric.low_confidence else max(0.0, confidence_metric.value * 0.5),
833	        max(0.0, 1.0 - contradiction_metric.value),
834	    ]
835	    confidence_scalar = (confidence_components[0] * confidence_components[1]) ** 0.5
836	    low_conf_penalty = 0.5 if any(m.low_confidence for m in metrics) else 1.0
837	    confidence_scalar = _clamp(confidence_scalar * low_conf_penalty, 0.0, 1.0)
838	    if action == MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN:
839	        confidence_scalar = min(confidence_scalar, 0.25)
840	
841	    stake_recommendation = _stake_recommendation(paper_bet, edge_metric, liquidity_metric)
842	    if action not in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE):
843	        stake_recommendation = None
844	
845	    return MarketDecisionTrace(
846	        action=action,
847	        side=side,
848	        confidence=confidence_scalar,
849	        stake_recommendation_usd=stake_recommendation,
850	        metrics=metrics,
851	        rules=rules,
852	        reasons=tuple(reasons),
853	    )
854	
855	
856	def decision_trace_to_dict(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> dict[str, Any]:
857	    """Public stable JSON shape for UI/API consumption."""
858	    return trace.to_dict()
859	
860	
861	def decision_trace_gate_entries(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
862	    """Render the rule-graph firings in the legacy ``gate_results`` shape.
863	
864	    The forecast_generator already persists a ``gate_results`` list with
865	    ``gateName/passed/reason`` keys; the rule graph's threshold/veto
866	    nodes naturally project onto the same shape so downstream
867	    consumers (operator page, e2e tests) keep working without a
868	    schema migration.
869	    """
870	    return [
871	        {
872	            "gateName": rule.name,
873	            "passed": bool(rule.passed),
874	            "reason": rule.detail,
875	        }
876	        for rule in trace.rules
877	        if rule.fired
878	    ]
879	
880	
881	__all__ = [
882	    "DecisionMetric",
883	    "DecisionRuleResult",
884	    "MarketDecisionAction",
885	    "MarketDecisionTrace",
886	    "TRACE_VERSION",
887	    "build_decision_trace",
888	    "compute_calibration_adjusted_confidence",
889	    "compute_contradiction_pressure",
890	    "compute_liquidity_cost_feasibility",
891	    "compute_market_mispricing_edge",
892	    "compute_source_domain_locality",
893	    "compute_temporal_decay_pressure",
894	    "decision_trace_gate_entries",
895	    "decision_trace_to_dict",
896	    "evaluate_rules",
897	]
898

"""Deterministic decision-metric and rule-graph engine for Forecasts.

This module implements the §3 catalog and §2.3 rule graph described in
``docs/architecture/Algorithmized_Decision_Making.md``. Inputs are the
artifacts already produced by ``forecast_generator`` (market row,
retrieved sources, validated citations, parsed LLM payload, optional
paper-bet record, optional calibration record). Outputs are a
typed :class:`MarketDecisionTrace` whose intermediate metric values,
rule firings, final action, and stake recommendation are reproducible
from those inputs alone — no further LLM calls, no randomness.

The trace is the investable artifact. Prose generated elsewhere is
allowed to describe it but never to override it: callers serialize the
trace into a stable JSON shape and persist it under
``ForecastTrace.model_output["decision_trace"]``.

Missing inputs degrade gracefully: metrics that cannot be computed are
recorded with ``low_confidence=True`` and a sentinel value, the
``confidence`` field on the trace is reduced, and the rule graph
refuses to escalate beyond ``WATCH`` rather than crashing.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from decimal import Decimal, InvalidOperation
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence


TRACE_VERSION = "decision_metrics@v1"

# Thresholds. Kept here (not env-driven) so the trace is reproducible
# from inputs alone; production overrides will move into a versioned
# rule-graph config per §2.3 of the design doc.
EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD = 0.05
EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD = 0.08
CONFIDENCE_LIVE_THRESHOLD = 0.55
LOCALITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD = 0.6
LIQUIDITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD = 0.5
CONTRADICTION_VETO = 0.5
DECAY_VETO = 0.9
DECAY_DOWNGRADE = 0.7
MIN_PAPER_STAKE_USD = 1.0
DEFAULT_PAPER_STAKE_USD = 10.0
SOURCE_AGE_REFERENCE = timedelta(days=18 * 31)


class MarketDecisionAction(str, Enum):
    """Investable outputs the rule graph may emit.

    The first version implements ABSTAIN, WATCH, PAPER_TRADE, and
    LIVE_CANDIDATE end-to-end. REDUCE and EXIT are reachable only when
    callers supply a non-empty ``position_state`` to
    :func:`build_decision_trace` (§6 prompt 20). HEDGE is intentionally
    omitted from v1 per §8 open questions.
    """

    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
    WATCH = "WATCH"
    PAPER_TRADE = "PAPER_TRADE"
    LIVE_CANDIDATE = "LIVE_CANDIDATE"
    REDUCE = "REDUCE"
    EXIT = "EXIT"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DecisionMetric:
    name: str
    value: float
    range_low: float
    range_high: float
    method: str
    low_confidence: bool = False
    detail: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "value": round(float(self.value), 6),
            "range": [self.range_low, self.range_high],
            "method": self.method,
            "low_confidence": bool(self.low_confidence),
            "detail": self.detail,
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DecisionRuleResult:
    name: str
    kind: str           # "threshold" | "veto" | "bucket" | "combiner"
    fired: bool         # rule was evaluated (vs short-circuited)
    passed: bool        # rule's predicate held
    detail: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "kind": self.kind,
            "fired": bool(self.fired),
            "passed": bool(self.passed),
            "detail": self.detail,
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MarketDecisionTrace:
    action: MarketDecisionAction
    side: str | None
    confidence: float
    stake_recommendation_usd: float | None
    metrics: tuple[DecisionMetric, ...]
    rules: tuple[DecisionRuleResult, ...]
    reasons: tuple[str, ...]
    trace_version: str = TRACE_VERSION

    def metric(self, name: str) -> DecisionMetric | None:
        for m in self.metrics:
            if m.name == name:
                return m
        return None

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "action": self.action.value,
            "side": self.side,
            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
            "stake_recommendation_usd": (
                None
                if self.stake_recommendation_usd is None
                else round(float(self.stake_recommendation_usd), 2)
            ),
            "metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.metrics],
            "rules": [r.to_dict() for r in self.rules],
            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
            "trace_version": self.trace_version,
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers


def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
    if value.tzinfo is None:
        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)


def _to_float(value: Any) -> float | None:
    if value is None or isinstance(value, bool):
        return None
    try:
        return float(value)
    except (TypeError, ValueError, InvalidOperation):
        return None


def _clamp(value: float, low: float, high: float) -> float:
    return max(low, min(high, value))


def _status_value(value: Any) -> str:
    return str(value.value if hasattr(value, "value") else value or "").upper()


def _source_created_at(source: Any) -> datetime | None:
    metadata = getattr(source, "metadata", {}) or {}
    for key in ("created_at", "createdAt", "episode_date", "updated_at"):
        raw = metadata.get(key) if isinstance(metadata, Mapping) else None
        if isinstance(raw, datetime):
            return _as_utc(raw)
        if isinstance(raw, str):
            try:
                parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(raw.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            except ValueError:
                continue
            return _as_utc(parsed)
    return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Metric calculators
#
# Every calculator is pure: same inputs → same metric. Calculators
# that lack their data return ``low_confidence=True`` with a sentinel
# value rather than raising.


def compute_market_mispricing_edge(
    probability_yes: Any,
    market_yes_price: Any,
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.9 — signed edge in [-1, 1]."""
    p = _to_float(probability_yes)
    market_p = _to_float(market_yes_price)
    if p is None or market_p is None or not 0.0 < market_p < 1.0:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="market_mispricing_edge",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=-1.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="edge_calc@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="missing or invalid probability/price",
        )
    edge = _clamp(p - market_p, -1.0, 1.0)
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="market_mispricing_edge",
        value=edge,
        range_low=-1.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="edge_calc@v1",
        detail=f"firm_p={p:.4f} market_p={market_p:.4f}",
    )


def compute_calibration_adjusted_confidence(
    confidence_low: Any,
    confidence_high: Any,
    calibration_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
    topic_hint: str | None = None,
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.8 — confidence (1 - interval width) mapped through per-domain
    calibration when available; otherwise the raw scalar with
    ``low_confidence=True`` so the rule graph refuses to escalate to live.
    """
    low = _to_float(confidence_low)
    high = _to_float(confidence_high)
    if low is None or high is None or low > high:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="calibration_adjusted_confidence",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="recalibration@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="confidence interval missing or inverted",
        )
    raw = _clamp(1.0 - (high - low), 0.0, 1.0)

    if not calibration_state:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="calibration_adjusted_confidence",
            value=raw,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="recalibration@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no active calibration record",
        )
    samples = int(calibration_state.get("min_samples", 0) or 0)
    threshold = int(calibration_state.get("recalibration_min_samples", 30) or 30)
    if samples < threshold:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="calibration_adjusted_confidence",
            value=raw,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="recalibration@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail=f"calibration samples {samples} < min {threshold}",
        )
    domain_map = calibration_state.get("domains") or {}
    adjustment = 1.0
    if topic_hint and isinstance(domain_map, Mapping):
        adjustment = float(domain_map.get(topic_hint, domain_map.get("__default__", 1.0)) or 1.0)
    adjusted = _clamp(raw * adjustment, 0.0, 1.0)
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="calibration_adjusted_confidence",
        value=adjusted,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="recalibration@v1",
        detail=f"raw={raw:.4f} factor={adjustment:.4f}",
    )


def compute_source_domain_locality(
    sources: Sequence[Any],
    citations: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
    market: Any,
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.4 — fraction of cited sources whose metadata-derived domain
    overlaps the market's category. Missing categories → low_confidence.
    """
    market_category = str(getattr(market, "category", "") or "").strip().lower()
    if not citations:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="source_domain_locality",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="domain_bounds@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no citations present",
        )
    by_id = {
        (str(getattr(s, "source_type", "")).upper(), str(getattr(s, "source_id", ""))): s
        for s in sources
    }
    if not market_category:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="source_domain_locality",
            value=0.5,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="domain_bounds@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="market has no category; locality cannot be tested",
        )
    in_bounds = 0
    judged = 0
    for citation in citations:
        key = (
            str(citation.get("source_type", "")).upper(),
            str(citation.get("source_id", "")),
        )
        source = by_id.get(key)
        if source is None:
            continue
        judged += 1
        metadata = getattr(source, "metadata", {}) or {}
        disciplines = metadata.get("disciplines") if isinstance(metadata, Mapping) else None
        terms: list[str] = []
        if isinstance(disciplines, (list, tuple)):
            terms.extend(str(d).lower() for d in disciplines)
        topic = metadata.get("topic_hint") if isinstance(metadata, Mapping) else None
        if isinstance(topic, str):
            terms.append(topic.lower())
        if not terms:
            continue
        if any(market_category in t or t in market_category for t in terms):
            in_bounds += 1
    if judged == 0:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="source_domain_locality",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="domain_bounds@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no cited sources resolved to retrieved sources",
        )
    value = in_bounds / judged
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="source_domain_locality",
        value=value,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="domain_bounds@v1",
        detail=f"{in_bounds}/{judged} citations in-bounds for category={market_category!r}",
    )


def compute_contradiction_pressure(
    citations: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
    extra_contradictions: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] = (),
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.2 — proxy: fraction of citations whose ``support_label`` is
    ``CONTRARY``, optionally widened by a caller-supplied
    ``extra_contradictions`` list (open contradictory conclusions
    matched at retrieval time).
    """
    total = len(citations) + len(extra_contradictions)
    if total == 0:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="contradiction_pressure",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="contradiction_probe@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no citations to evaluate",
        )
    contrary = sum(
        1
        for c in citations
        if str(c.get("support_label", "")).upper() == "CONTRARY"
    )
    contrary += len(extra_contradictions)
    value = _clamp(contrary / total, 0.0, 1.0)
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="contradiction_pressure",
        value=value,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="contradiction_probe@v1",
        detail=f"{contrary}/{total} contrary",
    )


def compute_liquidity_cost_feasibility(
    market: Any,
    suggested_stake_usd: float | None,
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.10 — 1 − fraction of stake consumed by spread/depth. Uses
    ``raw_payload['spread']`` or ``raw_payload['depth_usd']`` if present.
    Collapses to 0 when ``low_liquidity`` is True on the market mirror.
    """
    raw = getattr(market, "raw_payload", {}) or {}
    if not isinstance(raw, Mapping):
        raw = {}
    low_liquidity = bool(raw.get("low_liquidity", False))
    yes_price = _to_float(getattr(market, "current_yes_price", None))
    no_price = _to_float(getattr(market, "current_no_price", None))
    spread = _to_float(raw.get("spread"))
    if spread is None and yes_price is not None and no_price is not None:
        implied = yes_price + no_price
        if 0.5 < implied < 1.5:
            spread = abs(1.0 - implied)
    depth_usd = _to_float(raw.get("depth_usd"))
    if low_liquidity:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="liquidity_cost_feasibility",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="edge_calc@v1",
            detail="market mirror flagged low_liquidity",
        )
    if spread is None and depth_usd is None:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="liquidity_cost_feasibility",
            value=0.5,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="edge_calc@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no spread or depth data on market.raw_payload",
        )
    stake = suggested_stake_usd if suggested_stake_usd and suggested_stake_usd > 0 else DEFAULT_PAPER_STAKE_USD
    cost_fraction = 0.0
    detail_parts: list[str] = []
    if spread is not None:
        spread_cost = max(0.0, spread) * stake
        cost_fraction += spread_cost / stake
        detail_parts.append(f"spread={spread:.4f}")
    if depth_usd is not None and depth_usd > 0:
        depth_penalty = max(0.0, stake - depth_usd) / stake
        cost_fraction += depth_penalty
        detail_parts.append(f"depth_usd={depth_usd:.2f}")
    feasibility = _clamp(1.0 - cost_fraction, 0.0, 1.0)
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="liquidity_cost_feasibility",
        value=feasibility,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="edge_calc@v1",
        detail=" ".join(detail_parts) or "computed",
    )


def compute_temporal_decay_pressure(
    sources: Sequence[Any],
    market: Any,
    now: datetime | None = None,
) -> DecisionMetric:
    """§3.7 — weighted age of sources, normalized by either the
    market's time-to-resolution (preferred) or by
    ``SOURCE_AGE_REFERENCE`` (fallback). Higher = more decayed.
    """
    if not sources:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="temporal_decay_pressure",
            value=1.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="retrieval_adapter@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no retrieved sources",
        )
    now = _as_utc(now or datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    close = getattr(market, "close_time", None)
    if isinstance(close, datetime):
        horizon = max(timedelta(days=1), _as_utc(close) - now)
    else:
        horizon = SOURCE_AGE_REFERENCE
    ages: list[float] = []
    for source in sources:
        created = _source_created_at(source)
        if created is None:
            continue
        age = (now - created).total_seconds()
        if age < 0:
            age = 0.0
        ages.append(age)
    if not ages:
        return DecisionMetric(
            name="temporal_decay_pressure",
            value=0.5,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method="retrieval_adapter@v1",
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no source timestamps",
        )
    mean_age_days = (sum(ages) / len(ages)) / 86400.0
    horizon_days = horizon.total_seconds() / 86400.0
    pressure = _clamp(mean_age_days / max(1.0, horizon_days), 0.0, 1.0)
    return DecisionMetric(
        name="temporal_decay_pressure",
        value=pressure,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method="retrieval_adapter@v1",
        detail=f"mean_age_days={mean_age_days:.1f} horizon_days={horizon_days:.1f}",
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rule graph


def _market_is_closed(market: Any, now: datetime) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    status = _status_value(getattr(market, "status", ""))
    if status and status != "OPEN":
        return True, f"market status={status}"
    close = getattr(market, "close_time", None)
    if isinstance(close, datetime):
        if _as_utc(close) <= now:
            return True, "market.close_time has passed"
    return False, "market is open"


def _position_state(payload: Any) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
    if isinstance(payload, Mapping):
        return payload
    return {}


def _stake_recommendation(
    paper_bet: Any,
    edge: DecisionMetric,
    liquidity: DecisionMetric,
) -> float | None:
    if paper_bet is not None:
        stake = getattr(paper_bet, "stake_usd", None)
        if stake is not None:
            try:
                stake_f = float(Decimal(str(stake)))
            except (InvalidOperation, TypeError, ValueError):
                stake_f = None
            if stake_f is not None and stake_f >= MIN_PAPER_STAKE_USD:
                return stake_f
    if liquidity.low_confidence or liquidity.value <= 0:
        return None
    if abs(edge.value) < EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD or edge.low_confidence:
        return None
    return DEFAULT_PAPER_STAKE_USD


def evaluate_rules(
    metrics: Sequence[DecisionMetric],
    *,
    market_closed_reason: str | None,
    sources_count: int,
    min_distinct_sources: int,
    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None,
) -> tuple[tuple[DecisionRuleResult, ...], MarketDecisionAction, str | None, list[str]]:
    """Evaluate the §2.3 rule graph and return (rules, action, side, reasons).

    Ordering matters: vetoes are evaluated first and short-circuit
    later rules (their ``fired`` is False). Bucket rules then choose
    a candidate action which threshold/combiner rules may downgrade.
    """
    metric_by_name = {m.name: m for m in metrics}
    edge = metric_by_name["market_mispricing_edge"]
    confidence = metric_by_name["calibration_adjusted_confidence"]
    locality = metric_by_name["source_domain_locality"]
    contradiction = metric_by_name["contradiction_pressure"]
    liquidity = metric_by_name["liquidity_cost_feasibility"]
    decay = metric_by_name["temporal_decay_pressure"]

    rules: list[DecisionRuleResult] = []
    reasons: list[str] = []
    veto_hit = False

    # Veto 1: market closed/expired
    rule_closed = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="market_open",
        kind="veto",
        fired=True,
        passed=market_closed_reason is None,
        detail=market_closed_reason or "open",
    )
    rules.append(rule_closed)
    if not rule_closed.passed:
        reasons.append(rule_closed.detail)
        veto_hit = True

    # Veto 2: insufficient distinct sources
    rule_sources = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="distinct_source_floor",
        kind="veto",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=sources_count >= min_distinct_sources,
        detail=f"{sources_count}/{min_distinct_sources} distinct sources",
    )
    rules.append(rule_sources)
    if rule_sources.fired and not rule_sources.passed:
        reasons.append(rule_sources.detail)
        veto_hit = True

    # Veto 3: high contradiction pressure
    rule_contradict = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="contradiction_veto",
        kind="veto",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=contradiction.value < CONTRADICTION_VETO,
        detail=f"contradiction_pressure={contradiction.value:.3f} vs τ={CONTRADICTION_VETO}",
    )
    rules.append(rule_contradict)
    if rule_contradict.fired and not rule_contradict.passed:
        reasons.append(rule_contradict.detail)
        veto_hit = True

    # Veto 4: temporal decay catastrophic
    rule_decay_veto = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="temporal_decay_veto",
        kind="veto",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=decay.value < DECAY_VETO,
        detail=f"temporal_decay_pressure={decay.value:.3f} vs τ={DECAY_VETO}",
    )
    rules.append(rule_decay_veto)
    if rule_decay_veto.fired and not rule_decay_veto.passed:
        reasons.append(rule_decay_veto.detail)
        veto_hit = True

    # Bucket: edge magnitude
    abs_edge = abs(edge.value) if not edge.low_confidence else 0.0
    side = "YES" if edge.value >= 0 else "NO"
    if veto_hit:
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
        bucket_detail = "veto upstream → abstain"
        bucket_fired = False
    elif edge.low_confidence:
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
        bucket_detail = "edge data missing → abstain"
        bucket_fired = True
    elif abs_edge < EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD:
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH
        bucket_detail = f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} below paper threshold {EDGE_PAPER_THRESHOLD}"
        bucket_fired = True
    elif abs_edge < EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD:
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE
        bucket_detail = (
            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} clears paper, below live threshold {EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
        )
        bucket_fired = True
    else:
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE
        bucket_detail = f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} clears live threshold {EDGE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
        bucket_fired = True
    rules.append(
        DecisionRuleResult(
            name="edge_bucket",
            kind="bucket",
            fired=bucket_fired,
            passed=bucket_fired,
            detail=bucket_detail,
        )
    )

    # Liquidity gate (downgrades LIVE_CANDIDATE → PAPER_TRADE,
    # downgrades PAPER_TRADE → WATCH when liquidity is zero).
    liquidity_ok = liquidity.value >= LIQUIDITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not liquidity.low_confidence
    rule_liquidity = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="liquidity_threshold",
        kind="threshold",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=liquidity_ok,
        detail=f"liquidity_cost_feasibility={liquidity.value:.3f} vs τ={LIQUIDITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD}",
    )
    rules.append(rule_liquidity)
    if rule_liquidity.fired:
        if liquidity.value <= 0.0 and not liquidity.low_confidence:
            if bucket_action in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE):
                reasons.append("liquidity collapsed → watch")
                bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH
        elif not liquidity_ok and bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE:
            reasons.append("liquidity below live threshold → paper")
            bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE

    # Confidence/locality gate for live escalation only.
    confidence_ok = (
        confidence.value >= CONFIDENCE_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not confidence.low_confidence
    )
    locality_ok = locality.value >= LOCALITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD and not locality.low_confidence
    rule_conf = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="confidence_threshold",
        kind="threshold",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=confidence_ok,
        detail=(
            f"calibration_adjusted_confidence={confidence.value:.3f} vs τ={CONFIDENCE_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
            + (" (low_confidence)" if confidence.low_confidence else "")
        ),
    )
    rule_locality = DecisionRuleResult(
        name="locality_threshold",
        kind="threshold",
        fired=not veto_hit,
        passed=locality_ok,
        detail=(
            f"source_domain_locality={locality.value:.3f} vs τ={LOCALITY_LIVE_THRESHOLD}"
            + (" (low_confidence)" if locality.low_confidence else "")
        ),
    )
    rules.append(rule_conf)
    rules.append(rule_locality)
    if bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE:
        if not (confidence_ok and locality_ok):
            reasons.append("confidence or locality below live thresholds → paper")
            bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE

    # Moderate decay downgrade
    if decay.value >= DECAY_DOWNGRADE and bucket_action in (
        MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE,
        MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE,
    ):
        reasons.append(
            f"temporal_decay_pressure={decay.value:.3f} ≥ {DECAY_DOWNGRADE} → watch"
        )
        bucket_action = MarketDecisionAction.WATCH

    # Position-state overlays. v1 only emits REDUCE/EXIT when the
    # caller declares an open position whose side disagrees with the
    # current edge or whose underlying principle is revoked.
    state = _position_state(position_state)
    if state:
        open_side = str(state.get("open_side", "") or "").upper()
        revoked = bool(state.get("principles_revoked", False))
        position_action: MarketDecisionAction | None = None
        position_detail = ""
        if revoked:
            position_action = MarketDecisionAction.EXIT
            position_detail = "an active principle was revoked → exit"
        elif open_side and open_side != side and not edge.low_confidence:
            position_action = MarketDecisionAction.REDUCE
            position_detail = (
                f"existing {open_side} position vs edge side {side} → reduce"
            )
        rules.append(
            DecisionRuleResult(
                name="position_overlay",
                kind="combiner",
                fired=True,
                passed=position_action is not None,
                detail=position_detail or "no overlay applied",
            )
        )
        if position_action is not None:
            reasons.append(position_detail)
            bucket_action = position_action

    if bucket_action == MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN and not reasons:
        reasons.append("rule graph produced no actionable decision")
    if bucket_action in (MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN,) or veto_hit:
        side_out = None
    else:
        side_out = side
    return tuple(rules), bucket_action, side_out, reasons


def build_decision_trace(
    *,
    market: Any,
    sources: Sequence[Any],
    citations: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
    paper_bet: Any | None = None,
    calibration_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    extra_contradictions: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]] = (),
    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    now: datetime | None = None,
    min_distinct_sources: int = 3,
) -> MarketDecisionTrace:
    """Assemble metrics, evaluate the rule graph, return a trace.

    The trace's ``confidence`` is the geometric mean of the
    calibration-adjusted confidence and ``(1 - contradiction_pressure)``,
    pulled down by ``low_confidence`` metrics so missing data is
    visible in the scalar that the UI surfaces.
    """
    now = _as_utc(now or datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    probability_yes = payload.get("probability_yes")
    market_yes_price = getattr(market, "current_yes_price", None)

    edge_metric = compute_market_mispricing_edge(probability_yes, market_yes_price)
    confidence_metric = compute_calibration_adjusted_confidence(
        payload.get("confidence_low"),
        payload.get("confidence_high"),
        calibration_state,
        topic_hint=str(payload.get("topic_hint") or "").strip() or None,
    )
    locality_metric = compute_source_domain_locality(sources, citations, market)
    contradiction_metric = compute_contradiction_pressure(citations, extra_contradictions)
    decay_metric = compute_temporal_decay_pressure(sources, market, now=now)

    suggested_stake: float | None = None
    if paper_bet is not None:
        raw_stake = getattr(paper_bet, "stake_usd", None)
        if raw_stake is not None:
            try:
                suggested_stake = float(Decimal(str(raw_stake)))
            except (InvalidOperation, TypeError, ValueError):
                suggested_stake = None
    liquidity_metric = compute_liquidity_cost_feasibility(market, suggested_stake)

    metrics: tuple[DecisionMetric, ...] = (
        edge_metric,
        confidence_metric,
        locality_metric,
        contradiction_metric,
        liquidity_metric,
        decay_metric,
    )

    closed, closed_reason = _market_is_closed(market, now)
    rules, action, side, reasons = evaluate_rules(
        metrics,
        market_closed_reason=closed_reason if closed else None,
        sources_count=len(sources),
        min_distinct_sources=min_distinct_sources,
        position_state=position_state,
    )

    confidence_components = [
        confidence_metric.value if not confidence_metric.low_confidence else max(0.0, confidence_metric.value * 0.5),
        max(0.0, 1.0 - contradiction_metric.value),
    ]
    confidence_scalar = (confidence_components[0] * confidence_components[1]) ** 0.5
    low_conf_penalty = 0.5 if any(m.low_confidence for m in metrics) else 1.0
    confidence_scalar = _clamp(confidence_scalar * low_conf_penalty, 0.0, 1.0)
    if action == MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN:
        confidence_scalar = min(confidence_scalar, 0.25)

    stake_recommendation = _stake_recommendation(paper_bet, edge_metric, liquidity_metric)
    if action not in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE):
        stake_recommendation = None

    return MarketDecisionTrace(
        action=action,
        side=side,
        confidence=confidence_scalar,
        stake_recommendation_usd=stake_recommendation,
        metrics=metrics,
        rules=rules,
        reasons=tuple(reasons),
    )


def decision_trace_to_dict(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Public stable JSON shape for UI/API consumption."""
    return trace.to_dict()


def decision_trace_gate_entries(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Render the rule-graph firings in the legacy ``gate_results`` shape.

    The forecast_generator already persists a ``gate_results`` list with
    ``gateName/passed/reason`` keys; the rule graph's threshold/veto
    nodes naturally project onto the same shape so downstream
    consumers (operator page, e2e tests) keep working without a
    schema migration.
    """
    return [
        {
            "gateName": rule.name,
            "passed": bool(rule.passed),
            "reason": rule.detail,
        }
        for rule in trace.rules
        if rule.fired
    ]


__all__ = [
    "DecisionMetric",
    "DecisionRuleResult",
    "MarketDecisionAction",
    "MarketDecisionTrace",
    "TRACE_VERSION",
    "build_decision_trace",
    "compute_calibration_adjusted_confidence",
    "compute_contradiction_pressure",
    "compute_liquidity_cost_feasibility",
    "compute_market_mispricing_edge",
    "compute_source_domain_locality",
    "compute_temporal_decay_pressure",
    "decision_trace_gate_entries",
    "decision_trace_to_dict",
    "evaluate_rules",
]

1	"""Forecast retrieval adapter over the shared Noosphere retrieval path.
2	
3	The Forecasts pipeline retrieves from the same Noosphere index as Currents,
4	but its inputs and stricter surfacing rules differ. Centralizing this wrapper
5	means a future change to Currents-side retrieval cannot silently weaken
6	Forecasts-side surfacing -- the filter is enforced here, post-retrieval, where
7	Forecasts owns it.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
14	from typing import Any, Literal
15	
16	from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import retrieve_for_event
17	from noosphere.models import Claim, Conclusion, ForecastMarket
18	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
19	
20	log = get_logger(__name__)
21	
22	SourceType = Literal["CONCLUSION", "CLAIM"]
23	Visibility = Literal["PUBLIC", "FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"]
24	
25	DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8
26	RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE = 4
27	MAX_CANDIDATES = 40
28	MAX_SOURCE_AGE = timedelta(days=18 * 31)
29	MMR_LAMBDA = 0.7
30	
31	
32	@dataclass(frozen=True)
33	class RetrievedSource:
34	    source_type: SourceType
35	    source_id: str
36	    text: str
37	    relevance: float
38	    surfaceable: bool
39	    visibility: Visibility
40	    metadata: dict[str, Any]
41	
42	
43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
44	class _MarketRetrievalEvent:
45	    text: str
46	    topic_hint: str | None = None
47	    embedding: bytes | None = None
48	
49	
50	def _truncate(value: Any, limit: int) -> str:
51	    return str(value or "").strip()[:limit]
52	
53	
54	def _clean(value: Any) -> str:
55	    return str(value or "").strip()
56	
57	
58	def build_query_from_market(market: ForecastMarket) -> str:
59	    """
60	    Compose a single query string the embedding-side retriever can use.
61	
62	    Recipe:
63	      title
64	      "\n\n"
65	      description (truncated to 600 chars)
66	      "\n\n"
67	      "Resolution criteria: " + resolutionCriteria (truncated to 400 chars)
68	      "\n\n"
69	      "Category: " + category
70	    """
71	
72	    title = _clean(getattr(market, "title", ""))
73	    description = _truncate(getattr(market, "description", ""), 600)
74	    resolution_criteria = _truncate(
75	        getattr(
76	            market,
77	            "resolution_criteria",
78	            getattr(market, "resolutionCriteria", ""),
79	        ),
80	        400,
81	    )
82	    category = _clean(getattr(market, "category", ""))
83	    return (
84	        f"{title}\n\n"
85	        f"{description}\n\n"
86	        f"Resolution criteria: {resolution_criteria}\n\n"
87	        f"Category: {category}"
88	    )
89	
90	
91	def retrieve_for_market(
92	    store: Any,
93	    market: ForecastMarket,
94	    *,
95	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K,
96	) -> list[RetrievedSource]:
97	    """
98	    Wrap Currents retrieval for Forecast markets.
99	
100	    Filter rules:
101	      - drop sources with `visibility != PUBLIC` UNLESS source.surfaceable is True
102	      - drop revoked conclusions
103	      - drop sources older than 18 months unless `is_load_bearing` is True
104	      - apply MMR (maximal marginal relevance) with lambda=0.7 to diversify
105	      - return at most top_k sources
106	
107	    On error: never raise. Return [] and emit a `forecasts.retrieval.error`
108	    structured log. The generator treats [] as INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES.
109	    """
110	
111	    if top_k <= 0:
112	        return []
113	
114	    query = build_query_from_market(market)
115	    event = _MarketRetrievalEvent(
116	        text=query,
117	        topic_hint=getattr(market, "category", None),
118	    )
119	    retrieval_k = min(max(top_k * RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE, top_k), MAX_CANDIDATES)
120	
121	    try:
122	        hits = retrieve_for_event(store, event, top_k=retrieval_k)
123	        sources: list[RetrievedSource] = []
124	        seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
125	        for hit in hits:
126	            source = _source_from_hit(store, hit)
127	            if source is None:
128	                continue
129	            key = (source.source_type, source.source_id)
130	            if key in seen:
131	                continue
132	            seen.add(key)
133	            sources.append(source)
134	        return _mmr_select(sources, query, top_k=top_k)
135	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised via tests.
136	        log.warning(
137	            "forecasts.retrieval.error",
138	            market_id=getattr(market, "id", ""),
139	            error=str(exc),
140	        )
141	        return []
142	
143	
144	def _source_from_hit(store: Any, hit: Any) -> RetrievedSource | None:
145	    source_kind = str(getattr(hit, "source_kind", "")).lower()
146	    source_id = str(getattr(hit, "source_id", ""))
147	    if source_kind == "conclusion":
148	        conclusion = _get_conclusion(store, source_id)
149	        if conclusion is None or _is_revoked(conclusion):
150	            return None
151	        return _to_retrieved_source(
152	            source_type="CONCLUSION",
153	            source_id=source_id,
154	            text=str(getattr(hit, "text", getattr(conclusion, "text", ""))),
155	            relevance=float(getattr(hit, "score", 0.0)),
156	            source=conclusion,
157	        )
158	    if source_kind == "claim":
159	        claim = store.get_claim(source_id) if hasattr(store, "get_claim") else None
160	        if claim is None:
161	            return None
162	        return _to_retrieved_source(
163	            source_type="CLAIM",
164	            source_id=source_id,
165	            text=str(getattr(hit, "text", getattr(claim, "text", ""))),
166	            relevance=float(getattr(hit, "score", 0.0)),
167	            source=claim,
168	        )
169	    return None
170	
171	
172	def _get_conclusion(store: Any, conclusion_id: str) -> Conclusion | None:
173	    if hasattr(store, "get_conclusion"):
174	        conclusion = store.get_conclusion(conclusion_id)
175	        if conclusion is not None:
176	            return conclusion
177	    if hasattr(store, "list_conclusions"):
178	        for conclusion in store.list_conclusions():
179	            if str(getattr(conclusion, "id", "")) == conclusion_id:
180	                return conclusion
181	    return None
182	
183	
184	def _to_retrieved_source(
185	    *,
186	    source_type: SourceType,
187	    source_id: str,
188	    text: str,
189	    relevance: float,
190	    source: Conclusion | Claim,
191	) -> RetrievedSource | None:
192	    visibility = _visibility_for(source, source_type)
193	    surfaceable = _surfaceable_for(source, visibility)
194	    if visibility != "PUBLIC" and not surfaceable:
195	        return None
196	    if _is_stale(source):
197	        return None
198	    return RetrievedSource(
199	        source_type=source_type,
200	        source_id=source_id,
201	        text=text,
202	        relevance=max(0.0, min(1.0, relevance)),
203	        surfaceable=surfaceable,
204	        visibility=visibility,
205	        metadata=_metadata_for(source, source_type),
206	    )
207	
208	
209	def _field(source: Any, name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
210	    if hasattr(source, name):
211	        return getattr(source, name)
212	    extra = getattr(source, "model_extra", None)
213	    if isinstance(extra, dict) and name in extra:
214	        return extra[name]
215	    pydantic_extra = getattr(source, "__pydantic_extra__", None)
216	    if isinstance(pydantic_extra, dict) and name in pydantic_extra:
217	        return pydantic_extra[name]
218	    if isinstance(source, dict):
219	        return source.get(name, default)
220	    return default
221	
222	
223	def _literal_name(value: Any) -> str:
224	    if hasattr(value, "name"):
225	        return str(value.name).upper()
226	    if hasattr(value, "value"):
227	        return str(value.value).upper()
228	    return str(value or "").upper()
229	
230	
231	def _visibility_for(source: Any, source_type: SourceType) -> Visibility:
232	    explicit = _literal_name(_field(source, "visibility", ""))
233	    if explicit in {"PUBLIC", "FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"}:
234	        return explicit  # type: ignore[return-value]
235	
236	    if source_type == "CLAIM":
237	        origin = _literal_name(_field(source, "claim_origin", ""))
238	        if origin in {"FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"}:
239	            return origin  # type: ignore[return-value]
240	        return "PUBLIC"
241	
242	    confidence_tier = _literal_name(_field(source, "confidence_tier", ""))
243	    if confidence_tier == "FOUNDER":
244	        return "FOUNDER"
245	    return "PUBLIC"
246	
247	
248	def _surfaceable_for(source: Any, visibility: Visibility) -> bool:
249	    raw = _field(source, "surfaceable", None)
250	    if raw is not None:
251	        return bool(raw)
252	    public_safe = _field(source, "public_safe", None)
253	    if public_safe is not None:
254	        return bool(public_safe)
255	    return visibility == "PUBLIC"
256	
257	
258	def _is_revoked(source: Any) -> bool:
259	    if bool(_field(source, "is_revoked", False)):
260	        return True
261	    if _field(source, "revoked_at", None) is not None:
262	        return True
263	    if _field(source, "revokedAt", None) is not None:
264	        return True
265	    return False
266	
267	
268	def _is_stale(source: Any) -> bool:
269	    if bool(_field(source, "is_load_bearing", False)):
270	        return False
271	    created = _source_datetime(source)
272	    if created is None:
273	        return False
274	    return _utc_now() - created > MAX_SOURCE_AGE
275	
276	
277	def _source_datetime(source: Any) -> datetime | None:
278	    for field_name in (
279	        "created_at",
280	        "createdAt",
281	        "episode_date",
282	        "updated_at",
283	        "updatedAt",
284	    ):
285	        value = _field(source, field_name, None)
286	        if isinstance(value, datetime):
287	            return _as_utc(value)
288	        if isinstance(value, date):
289	            return datetime(value.year, value.month, value.day, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
290	    return None
291	
292	
293	def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
294	    if value.tzinfo is None:
295	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
296	    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
297	
298	
299	def _utc_now() -> datetime:
300	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
301	
302	
303	def _metadata_for(source: Any, source_type: SourceType) -> dict[str, Any]:
304	    metadata: dict[str, Any] = {
305	        "source_type": source_type,
306	    }
307	    for field_name in (
308	        "disciplines",
309	        "confidence",
310	        "confidence_tier",
311	        "principles_used",
312	        "claims_used",
313	        "claim_origin",
314	        "source_id",
315	        "chunk_id",
316	        "evidence_pointers",
317	        "is_load_bearing",
318	    ):
319	        value = _field(source, field_name, None)
320	        if value is None:
321	            continue
322	        metadata[field_name] = _serializable(value)
323	    return metadata
324	
325	
326	def _serializable(value: Any) -> Any:
327	    if isinstance(value, list):
328	        return [_serializable(item) for item in value]
329	    if hasattr(value, "value"):
330	        return value.value
331	    if isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, dict)):
332	        return value
333	    return str(value)
334	
335	
336	def _mmr_select(
337	    sources: list[RetrievedSource],
338	    query: str,
339	    *,
340	    top_k: int,
341	) -> list[RetrievedSource]:
342	    if len(sources) <= top_k:
343	        return sorted(sources, key=_rank_key)[:top_k]
344	
345	    selected: list[RetrievedSource] = []
346	    remaining = sorted(sources, key=_rank_key)
347	    query_terms = _terms(query)
348	    while remaining and len(selected) < top_k:
349	        best = max(
350	            remaining,
351	            key=lambda source: _mmr_score(source, selected, query_terms),
352	        )
353	        selected.append(best)
354	        remaining.remove(best)
355	    return selected
356	
357	
358	def _mmr_score(
359	    source: RetrievedSource,
360	    selected: list[RetrievedSource],
361	    query_terms: set[str],
362	) -> float:
363	    query_similarity = max(source.relevance, _jaccard(_terms(source.text), query_terms))
364	    if not selected:
365	        return query_similarity
366	    diversity_penalty = max(
367	        _jaccard(_terms(source.text), _terms(chosen.text)) for chosen in selected
368	    )
369	    return MMR_LAMBDA * query_similarity - (1.0 - MMR_LAMBDA) * diversity_penalty
370	
371	
372	def _rank_key(source: RetrievedSource) -> tuple[float, int, str]:
373	    return (
374	        -source.relevance,
375	        0 if source.source_type == "CONCLUSION" else 1,
376	        source.source_id,
377	    )
378	
379	
380	def _terms(text: str) -> set[str]:
381	    return {
382	        token
383	        for token in "".join(ch.lower() if ch.isalnum() else " " for ch in text).split()
384	        if len(token) >= 3
385	    }
386	
387	
388	def _jaccard(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
389	    if not left or not right:
390	        return 0.0
391	    return len(left & right) / len(left | right)
392

"""Principle abstraction pass — consumes case extractions, emits candidate
principles, and assembles a transfer graph.

The pass is deliberately *not* a clustering routine. Two cases
abstract to the same principle iff they link to the same canonical
statement (modulo whitespace and casing). Semantic clustering of
similar-but-not-identical principle texts is a job for the
distillation pipeline (:mod:`noosphere.distillation`), which already
gates on cross-domain breadth.

What this pass does:

1. Walk ``CaseStudyExtraction`` records.
2. For each ``EmpiricalCaseStudy.linked_principles`` entry, derive a
   content-addressed principle id and either create a new
   :class:`AbstractPrinciple` or extend an existing one with the
   new case as a supporting node.
3. Accept *abstract-only* sources (e.g. a passage where the author
   states a principle without attaching a concrete case). These
   produce a principle with provenance to the chunk but no
   ``CASE_INSTANTIATES`` edge.
4. Allow the caller to record bounding / contradicting cases
   explicitly; the abstractor cannot infer them from a single
   ``EmpiricalCaseStudy``, but it knows how to register them.
5. Emit a :class:`TransferGraph` with typed edges.

The pass is conservative on confidence:

- a single case → ``CANDIDATE`` (low confidence band);
- two or more independent supporting cases (different chunk_ids) →
  ``REFINED`` (moderate);
- at least one contradicting case → ``CONTRADICTED``;
- at least one bounding case → ``BOUNDED`` (overrides ``REFINED``
  but not ``CONTRADICTED``);
- the band never crosses into ``HIGH``: that is reserved for the
  distillation path with cross-domain corroboration.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Iterable, Optional, Sequence

from noosphere.cases.models import (
    AbstractPrincipleLink,
    CaseStudyExtraction,
    EmpiricalCaseStudy,
)
from noosphere.principles.models import (
    AbstractPrinciple,
    ConfidenceCalibration,
    FailureCondition,
    NegationCandidate,
    PrincipleConfidence,
    PrincipleProvenance,
    PrincipleStatus,
    TransferEdge,
    TransferEdgeKind,
    TransferGraph,
    TransferRisk,
    canonical_principle_id,
    normalize_principle_text,
)


# ── Inputs the caller may supply alongside case extractions ──────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AbstractOnlySource:
    """A passage that states a principle *without* attaching a concrete case.

    Created when the case extractor emits a ``NonCaseMention`` of
    kind ``ABSTRACT_CONCEPT`` *and* the surrounding pipeline has
    enough additional structure (mechanism, preconditions, expected
    outcome, failure conditions) to make the principle
    contradiction-testable. The abstractor does not synthesize these
    fields from prose — they must be supplied by the caller, because
    inventing them silently is precisely the failure mode the package
    contract refuses.
    """

    canonical_statement: str
    mechanism: str
    chunk_id: str = ""
    source_quote: str = ""
    domain: str = ""
    scope: Sequence[str] = ()
    preconditions: Sequence[str] = ()
    expected_outcomes: Sequence[str] = ()
    failure_conditions: Sequence[FailureCondition] = ()
    negation_candidates: Sequence[NegationCandidate] = ()
    transfer_risk: Optional[TransferRisk] = None
    notes: str = ""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BoundingCaseLink:
    """A directive: case ``case_id`` *bounds* principle ``principle_id``.

"""Abstract, contradiction-testable principles and a case<->principle transfer graph.

Where ``noosphere.cases`` extracts *concrete observed situations* and
``noosphere.distillation`` clusters *firm-level convictions* off of
conclusions, this package occupies the middle layer:

- a **principle abstraction** is the reusable logic a case (or several
  cases) instantiates — a mechanism with named preconditions, expected
  outcomes, explicit failure conditions, and explicit negation
  candidates that make it falsifiable;

- a **transfer graph** records how principles relate to one another
  (``contradicts``, ``refines``, ``generalizes``, ``bounds``) and how
  cases relate to principles (``instantiates``, ``bounds``,
  ``contradicts``).

The package deliberately refuses two short-cuts:

1. Not every claim is a principle. A passage that asserts "X causes Y"
   without a transferable mechanism is not promoted.
2. Multiple supporting cases do not, by themselves, promote a
   principle to firm-level confidence. Promotion is a separate
   concern (see :mod:`noosphere.distillation`); this package caps
   confidence at ``REFINED`` and surfaces calibration / transfer-risk
   fields for an external rater to consult.
"""

from noosphere.principles.models import (
    AbstractPrinciple,
    ConfidenceCalibration,
    FailureCondition,
    NegationCandidate,
    PrincipleConfidence,
    PrincipleProvenance,
    PrincipleStatus,
    TransferEdge,
    TransferEdgeKind,
    TransferGraph,
    TransferRisk,
    canonical_principle_id,
    normalize_principle_text,
)
from noosphere.principles.abstractor import (
    PrincipleAbstractor,
    PrincipleAbstractionResult,
)

__all__ = [
    "AbstractPrinciple",
    "ConfidenceCalibration",
    "FailureCondition",
    "NegationCandidate",
    "PrincipleAbstractionResult",
    "PrincipleAbstractor",
    "PrincipleConfidence",
    "PrincipleProvenance",
    "PrincipleStatus",
    "TransferEdge",
    "TransferEdgeKind",
    "TransferGraph",
    "TransferRisk",
    "canonical_principle_id",
    "normalize_principle_text",
]

100	
101	    Recorded by hand (or by a downstream reviewer); the abstractor
102	    does not detect bounds from prose alone.
103	    """
104	
105	    case_id: str
106	    principle_id: str
107	    rationale: str = ""
108	
109	
110	@dataclass(frozen=True)
111	class ContradictingCaseLink:
112	    """A directive: case ``case_id`` *contradicts* principle ``principle_id``."""
113	
114	    case_id: str
115	    principle_id: str
116	    rationale: str = ""
117	
118	
119	# ── Result ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	
121	
122	@dataclass
123	class PrincipleAbstractionResult:
124	    """What the abstractor returns: the assembled graph plus a list of
125	    principles that the caller should consider for review.
126	
127	    Returning the graph and the principle list separately keeps the
128	    common case (write to disk, render to UI) ergonomic while
129	    preserving the typed graph for downstream traversal.
130	    """
131	
132	    graph: TransferGraph
133	    principles: list[AbstractPrinciple]
134	    skipped_links: list[AbstractPrincipleLink]
135	
136	
137	# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138	
139	
140	def _link_is_contradiction_testable(link: AbstractPrincipleLink) -> bool:
141	    """An ``AbstractPrincipleLink`` produced by the case extractor only
142	    carries ``principle_text`` and ``transfer_conditions``. Neither field
143	    alone is sufficient to satisfy
144	    :class:`AbstractPrinciple`'s model-level requirement that a principle
145	    declare at least one failure condition or negation candidate. The
146	    caller (or the extractor's prompt) is expected to populate one of
147	    them via :func:`build_principle_from_link`'s ``failure_conditions`` /
148	    ``negation_candidates`` hooks. This helper just guards the link
149	    has *some* principle text to work from.
150	    """
151	
152	    return bool(link.principle_text.strip())
153	
154	
155	def _failure_condition_from_transfer_condition(
156	    transfer_condition: str,
157	) -> Optional[FailureCondition]:
158	    """Promote a non-empty ``transfer_conditions`` string into a failure
159	    condition.
160	
161	    The case extractor's ``transfer_conditions`` field captures *where*
162	    the principle is supposed to hold ("Applies whenever a leveraged
163	    intermediary depends on rolling short-term funding"). Inverted,
164	    that yields a usable failure-condition description ("Principle
165	    does not apply when ..."). This rewriting is conservative — we
166	    keep the original phrase and label it as a scope marker, leaving
167	    the rater to refine it.
168	    """
169	
170	    text = (transfer_condition or "").strip()
171	    if not text:
172	        return None
173	    return FailureCondition(
174	        description=(
175	            f"Principle should not be applied outside its stated scope: {text}"
176	        ),
177	        detectable_signal="Scope check: target case must satisfy the stated transfer conditions.",
178	        severity=PrincipleConfidence.MODERATE,
179	    )
180	
181	
182	def _confidence_band_for(
183	    supporting: int, contradicting: int, bounding: int, domain_breadth: int
184	) -> tuple[PrincipleConfidence, float]:
185	    """Map raw counts to a coarse band + continuous score.
186	
187	    The mapping is intentionally conservative:
188	
189	    - Score is capped at 0.7 (the upper end of the ``MODERATE`` band).
190	      Promotion past 0.7 is reserved for the cross-domain distillation
191	      path.
192	    - A contradicting case never produces a ``HIGH`` band even if
193	      supporting count is large.
194	    - Domain breadth nudges the score upward but cannot, by itself,
195	      override contradictions.
196	    """
197	
198	    if contradicting > 0 and supporting <= contradicting:
199	        return PrincipleConfidence.LOW, 0.2
200	
201	    base = 0.0
202	    if supporting >= 1:
203	        base = 0.3
204	    if supporting >= 2:
205	        base = 0.5
206	    if bounding >= 1:
207	        base = max(base, 0.45)
208	    if domain_breadth >= 2:
209	        base = min(0.7, base + 0.1)
210	    if contradicting >= 1:
211	        base = min(base, 0.4)
212	
213	    if base >= 0.5:
214	        return PrincipleConfidence.MODERATE, base
215	    return PrincipleConfidence.LOW, base
216	
217	
218	def _status_for(
219	    supporting: int, contradicting: int, bounding: int
220	) -> PrincipleStatus:
221	    if contradicting >= 1:
222	        return PrincipleStatus.CONTRADICTED
223	    if bounding >= 1:
224	        return PrincipleStatus.BOUNDED
225	    if supporting >= 2:
226	        return PrincipleStatus.REFINED
227	    return PrincipleStatus.CANDIDATE
228	
229	
230	# ── Principle builders ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
231	
232	
233	def build_principle_from_link(
234	    *,
235	    case: EmpiricalCaseStudy,
236	    chunk_id: str,
237	    link: AbstractPrincipleLink,
238	    additional_failure_conditions: Sequence[FailureCondition] = (),
239	    additional_negation_candidates: Sequence[NegationCandidate] = (),
240	    transfer_risk: Optional[TransferRisk] = None,
241	) -> Optional[AbstractPrinciple]:
242	    """Construct a single :class:`AbstractPrinciple` from one case→principle link.
243	
244	    Returns ``None`` if the link cannot be made contradiction-testable —
245	    i.e. neither a failure condition nor a negation candidate can be
246	    derived from the link's ``transfer_conditions`` and none were
247	    supplied by the caller. This is the path the *skipped_links* return
248	    field on :class:`PrincipleAbstractionResult` records.
249	    """
250	
251	    if not _link_is_contradiction_testable(link):
252	        return None
253	
254	    failure_conditions = list(additional_failure_conditions)
255	    derived_failure = _failure_condition_from_transfer_condition(
256	        link.transfer_conditions
257	    )
258	    if derived_failure is not None:
259	        failure_conditions.append(derived_failure)
260	    negation_candidates = list(additional_negation_candidates)
261	
262	    if not failure_conditions and not negation_candidates:
263	        # Without a stated failure mode or a negation candidate the
264	        # principle would not be contradiction-testable. We refuse to
265	        # fabricate one — the extractor is expected to either fill
266	        # ``transfer_conditions`` or to plumb explicit failure
267	        # conditions through the abstractor.
268	        return None
269	
270	    statement = link.principle_text.strip()
271	    principle_id = canonical_principle_id(statement)
272	    scope = [case.domain] if case.domain else []
273	    return AbstractPrinciple(
274	        id=principle_id,
275	        canonical_statement=statement,
276	        scope=scope,
277	        domain=case.domain,
278	        mechanism=case.observed_mechanism,
279	        preconditions=[],
280	        expected_outcomes=[case.outcome] if case.outcome else [],
281	        failure_conditions=failure_conditions,
282	        negation_candidates=negation_candidates,
283	        supporting_case_ids=[case.id],
284	        provenance=[
285	            PrincipleProvenance(
286	                chunk_id=chunk_id,
287	                source_quote=case.source_span.source_quote,
288	                case_id=case.id,
289	                extracted_from="case",
290	            )
291	        ],
292	        transfer_risk=transfer_risk or TransferRisk(),
293	    )
294	
295	
296	def build_principle_from_abstract_source(
297	    source: AbstractOnlySource,
298	) -> AbstractPrinciple:
299	    """Construct a principle from an abstract-only source.
300	
301	    The caller must supply at least one of ``failure_conditions`` or
302	    ``negation_candidates``; otherwise the :class:`AbstractPrinciple`
303	    validator refuses construction. We surface that as a ``ValueError``
304	    rather than silently dropping the source — the failure mode is
305	    "I cannot make this contradiction-testable", which deserves a
306	    loud diagnostic.
307	    """
308	
309	    statement = source.canonical_statement.strip()
310	    pid = canonical_principle_id(statement)
311	    return AbstractPrinciple(
312	        id=pid,
313	        canonical_statement=statement,
314	        scope=list(source.scope),
315	        domain=source.domain,
316	        mechanism=source.mechanism,
317	        preconditions=list(source.preconditions),
318	        expected_outcomes=list(source.expected_outcomes),
319	        failure_conditions=list(source.failure_conditions),
320	        negation_candidates=list(source.negation_candidates),
321	        supporting_case_ids=[],
322	        provenance=[
323	            PrincipleProvenance(
324	                chunk_id=source.chunk_id,
325	                source_quote=source.source_quote,
326	                case_id=None,
327	                extracted_from="abstract_only",
328	            )
329	        ],
330	        transfer_risk=source.transfer_risk or TransferRisk(),
331	        status=PrincipleStatus.CANDIDATE,
332	    )
333	
334	
335	# ── Abstractor ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
336	
337	
338	class PrincipleAbstractor:
339	    """Glue between case extractions and the transfer graph.
340	
341	    Usage::
342	
343	        abstractor = PrincipleAbstractor()
344	        result = abstractor.abstract(
345	            extractions=[extraction_a, extraction_b],
346	            abstract_only_sources=[abstract_source],
347	            bounding_links=[BoundingCaseLink(...)],
348	            contradicting_links=[ContradictingCaseLink(...)],
349	        )
350	        graph_json = result.graph.to_json()
351	
352	    The class itself is stateless; the caller is responsible for
353	    deciding which case extractions and overrides are in scope for a
354	    single pass. This makes the abstractor easy to compose into the
355	    Round 18 prompt batch without smuggling shared state.
356	    """
357	
358	    def abstract(
359	        self,
360	        *,
361	        extractions: Iterable[CaseStudyExtraction] = (),
362	        abstract_only_sources: Iterable[AbstractOnlySource] = (),
363	        bounding_links: Iterable[BoundingCaseLink] = (),
364	        contradicting_links: Iterable[ContradictingCaseLink] = (),
365	    ) -> PrincipleAbstractionResult:
366	        graph = TransferGraph()
367	        skipped: list[AbstractPrincipleLink] = []
368	
369	        # 1. Case-derived principles. Process in a deterministic order
370	        #    (by case id) so two runs over the same logical inputs in
371	        #    different insertion order produce byte-identical
372	        #    serialization. ``mechanism``, ``expected_outcomes``, and
373	        #    derived ``failure_conditions`` depend on the first case
374	        #    to instantiate a principle; the sort ensures "first" is
375	        #    a function of the data, not the caller's iteration order.
376	        sorted_cases: list[tuple[str, EmpiricalCaseStudy]] = []
377	        for extraction in extractions:
378	            chunk_id = extraction.chunk_id
379	            for case in extraction.cases:
380	                effective_chunk = chunk_id or case.source_span.chunk_id
381	                sorted_cases.append((effective_chunk, case))
382	        sorted_cases.sort(key=lambda pair: pair[1].id)
383	
384	        for chunk_id, case in sorted_cases:
385	            graph.add_case(case.id)
386	            for link in case.linked_principles:
387	                principle = build_principle_from_link(
388	                    case=case,
389	                    chunk_id=chunk_id,
390	                    link=link,
391	                )
392	                if principle is None:
393	                    skipped.append(link)
394	                    continue
395	                merged = graph.add_principle(principle)
396	                # ``add_principle`` merges supporting_case_ids; we
397	                # still need the edge so the graph traversal works.
398	                graph.add_edge(
399	                    TransferEdge(
